ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater (MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm) RMM tool deployment via phishing-delivered MSI
ucs.description: Hunts for MuddyWater's signature post-2022 tradecraft of dropping legitimate Remote Monitoring & Management agents (ScreenConnect/ConnectWise Control, Atera, RemoteUtilities, SimpleHelp, Syncro) via direct phishing of MSI installers — specifically the chain where Outlook/Office or msiexec installs an RMM client that hadn't previously existed on the host. Microsoft Threat Intelligence has tied this exact pattern to MERCURY/Mango Sandstorm (G0069) intrusions across Middle Eastern targets.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as child_cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as child_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","screenconnect.exe","ateraagent.exe","ateraadmin.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","remoteutilities.exe","simplehelp.exe","syncroservice.exe","kabuto.service.runner.exe","anydesk.exe") OR Processes.process_path IN ("*\\ScreenConnect\\*","*\\Atera*\\*","*\\RemoteUtilities\\*","*\\SimpleHelp\\*","*\\Syncro\\*")) AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("msiexec.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","explorer.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where match(parent_cmdline, "(?i)(https?://|/i\s+http|/package\s+http|\.msi)")
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let RmmBinaries = dynamic(["screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","screenconnect.exe","ateraagent.exe","ateraadmin.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","remoteutilities.exe","simplehelp.exe","syncroservice.exe","kabuto.service.runner.exe","anydesk.exe"]);
let RmmFolderTokens = dynamic([@"\ScreenConnect\", @"\Atera\", @"\RemoteUtilities\", @"\SimpleHelp\", @"\Syncro\"]);
let BaselineDays = 30d;
let RecentHours = 24h;
let Baseline = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(RecentHours))
    | where FileName in~ (RmmBinaries) or FolderPath has_any (RmmFolderTokens)
    | summarize by DeviceName, FileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(RecentHours)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (RmmBinaries) or FolderPath has_any (RmmFolderTokens)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("msiexec.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","explorer.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe")
   or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","/i ","/package ")
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on DeviceName, FileName    // first-time-seen on this host
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildPath = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let RmmBinaries = dynamic(["screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","screenconnect.exe","ateraagent.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","remoteutilities.exe","simplehelp.exe","syncroservice.exe","kabuto.service.runner.exe","anydesk.exe"]);
let RmmFolderTokens = dynamic([@"\ScreenConnect\", @"\Atera\", @"\RemoteUtilities\", @"\SimpleHelp\", @"\Syncro\"]);
let BaselineDays = 30d;
let RecentHours = 24h;
let Baseline = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(RecentHours))
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName has_any (RmmBinaries) or NewProcessName has_any (RmmFolderTokens)
    | summarize by Computer, NewProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentHours)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName has_any (RmmBinaries) or NewProcessName has_any (RmmFolderTokens)
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("msiexec.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","explorer.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe")
   or CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","/i ","/package ")
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on Computer, NewProcessName
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater RMM Tool Drop via Office/Msiexec Parent
id: 8a4d2b6c-9e1f-4f3a-9c2e-mw-rmm-001
status: experimental
description: Detects MuddyWater (G0069 / MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm) deploying legitimate RMM agents (ScreenConnect, Atera, RemoteUtilities, SimpleHelp, Syncro, AnyDesk) via phishing-delivered MSI installers, where the parent is Office, Outlook, msiexec, or a script host.
references:
    - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2022/08/25/mercury-leveraging-log4j-2-vulnerabilities-in-unpatched-systems-to-target-israeli-organizations/
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0069/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
    - attack.execution
    - attack.command-and-control
    - attack.t1219
    - attack.t1218.007
    - attack.t1204.002
    - attack.g0069
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_rmm_image:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\screenconnect.clientservice.exe'
            - '\screenconnect.windowsclient.exe'
            - '\screenconnect.exe'
            - '\ateraagent.exe'
            - '\rutserv.exe'
            - '\rfusclient.exe'
            - '\remoteutilities.exe'
            - '\simplehelp.exe'
            - '\syncroservice.exe'
            - '\kabuto.service.runner.exe'
            - '\anydesk.exe'
    selection_rmm_path:
        Image|contains:
            - '\ScreenConnect\'
            - '\Atera\'
            - '\RemoteUtilities\'
            - '\SimpleHelp\'
            - '\Syncro\'
    selection_parent:
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\msiexec.exe'
            - '\outlook.exe'
            - '\winword.exe'
            - '\excel.exe'
            - '\explorer.exe'
            - '\wscript.exe'
            - '\cscript.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
    condition: (selection_rmm_image or selection_rmm_path) and selection_parent
falsepositives:
    - Sanctioned IT support deployments — baseline known-good source IPs and admin accounts before alerting
level: high
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater pivoted from custom POWERSTATS/PowGoop tooling to abusing legitimate RMM software around 2022 — Microsoft (Mango Sandstorm), Mandiant, and Deep Instinct have all attributed direct phishing of MSI installers for ScreenConnect, Atera, RemoteUtilities, SimpleHelp, and Syncro to this group as their primary initial-access-to-persistence pivot in 2022-2024 campaigns against Middle Eastern, Is
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater LOLBin-launched obfuscated PowerShell stager (POWERSTATS / PhonyC2 chain)
ucs.description: Detects MuddyWater's signature first-stage execution pattern: a Windows signed-binary proxy (mshta, msiexec, wscript, cscript, rundll32, regsvr32) spawning powershell.exe with hallmark obfuscation flags — base64 EncodedCommand, hidden window, bypass execution policy, or in-memory download cradles. This is the launcher chain for POWERSTATS, Powermud, MuddyC3, and PhonyC2 backdoors and matches CISA AA22-055A / MAR-10369127 indicators.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as child_cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("mshta.exe","msiexec.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","hh.exe","cmstp.exe") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where match(child_cmdline, "(?i)(-enc(odedcommand)?\b|-e\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}|FromBase64String|-w\s+hidden|-windowstyle\s+h(idden)?|-ep\s+bypass|-executionpolicy\s+bypass|-nop\b|DownloadString|Invoke-Expression|\bIEX\b|Invoke-WebRequest|\bIWR\b|New-Object\s+Net\.WebClient)")
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("mshta.exe","msiexec.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","hh.exe","cmstp.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-enc","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","-w hidden","-WindowStyle Hidden","-ep bypass","-ExecutionPolicy Bypass","DownloadString","Invoke-Expression"," IEX ","Invoke-WebRequest"," IWR ","Net.WebClient")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-e(c|nc|ncodedcommand)?\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{40,}"
| extend B64 = extract(@"(?i)(?:-(?:e(?:nc(?:odedcommand)?)?))\s+([A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,})", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend Decoded = iif(isnotempty(B64), base64_decode_tostring(B64), "")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          B64, Decoded,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("\\mshta.exe","\\msiexec.exe","\\wscript.exe","\\cscript.exe","\\rundll32.exe","\\regsvr32.exe","\\hh.exe","\\cmstp.exe")
| where CommandLine has_any ("-enc","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","-w hidden","-WindowStyle Hidden","-ep bypass","-ExecutionPolicy Bypass","DownloadString","Invoke-Expression"," IEX ","Invoke-WebRequest"," IWR ","Net.WebClient")
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-e(c|nc|ncodedcommand)?\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{40,}"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater LOLBin-Spawned PowerShell Stager
id: 4f2b7d1a-c0e3-49b6-bd1f-mw-pwsh-002
status: experimental
description: Detects MuddyWater (G0069 / MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm / Static Kitten) characteristic POWERSTATS / PhonyC2 stager — mshta/msiexec/wscript/cscript/rundll32/regsvr32 spawning PowerShell with hidden-window, bypass-execution-policy, base64 EncodedCommand or in-memory download-cradle command lines.
references:
    - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-055a
    - https://www.deepinstinct.com/blog/phonyc2-revealing-a-new-malicious-command-control-framework-by-muddywater
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0069/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
    - attack.execution
    - attack.defense-evasion
    - attack.t1059.001
    - attack.t1218.005
    - attack.t1218.007
    - attack.t1027
    - attack.g0069
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_child:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '\pwsh.exe'
    selection_parent:
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\mshta.exe'
            - '\msiexec.exe'
            - '\wscript.exe'
            - '\cscript.exe'
            - '\rundll32.exe'
            - '\regsvr32.exe'
            - '\hh.exe'
            - '\cmstp.exe'
    selection_obf:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - '-enc'
            - '-EncodedCommand'
            - 'FromBase64String'
            - '-w hidden'
            - '-WindowStyle Hidden'
            - '-ep bypass'
            - '-ExecutionPolicy Bypass'
            - 'DownloadString'
            - 'Invoke-Expression'
            - 'IEX('
            - 'Invoke-WebRequest'
            - 'Net.WebClient'
    condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
    - Some software installers chain PowerShell post-install scripts via msiexec — baseline known installer SHA256s
level: high
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater's initial-access-to-execution pipeline has been remarkably consistent across CISA AA22-055A, Microsoft MERCURY reporting, and Deep Instinct's PhonyC2 disclosure: a malicious lure (HTA, MSI, VBS, JS, INF) spawns powershell.exe with -enc / -w hidden / -ep bypass to load POWERSTATS, PhonyC2, MuddyC3, or Small Sieve. The LOLBin-parent + PowerShell-child + obfuscation-flag triple combination
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
